themes.evidence: Goldman Sachs economists say the latest Fed signals and data argue for ‘later and fewer’ rate cuts than previously expected.
Bank of America strategists note that recent US activity data and PMIs remain consistent with ‘above-trend’ growth rather than an imminent downturn.
JPMorgan’s economics team highlights that core services and wage-sensitive components of inflation remain too high for the Fed to ease quickly.
UBS economists argue that softer payroll growth and slower wage gains point to a ‘gradual cooling’ rather than a sharp deterioration in the US labor market.
Citi FX strategists state that delayed Fed easing and relative US growth support a ‘firm-to-strong’ dollar backdrop versus major currencies.
Morgan Stanley Japan strategists expect the BOJ to proceed with ‘incremental tightening and reduced JGB purchases’ while avoiding disruptive moves.
Deutsche Bank strategists say the ECB is inclined toward a ‘very gradual’ cutting cycle given sticky core inflation and political risks in Europe.
Barclays credit strategy notes that HY and IG spreads remain near cycle tights, reflecting ‘ongoing demand for carry’ despite higher policy rates.
JPMorgan’s cross-asset team maintains a modest ‘overweight equities versus duration’ stance on the back of resilient earnings and data.
Citi strategists argue that ongoing US–China tensions and Middle East risks justify a ‘persistent geopolitical risk premium’ in commodities and FX.
